content: Sven: By the Nine, your eyes... blue as the icy veins of Winterhold. Tell me, do all vampires have such captivating gaze, or are you just especially blessed by Dibella's cruel sense of humor? Serana: Oh darling, if you think my eyes are impressive, you should see what my fangs can do to a man's neck... and other places. But tell me, little bard, why waste your breath on me when sweet Camilla's heart still dangles just out of reach? Sven: That damned Faendal has her bewitched, I swear it. You'd think an elf would stick to trees, not try and climb his way into proper Nord women's beds. Serana: Mmm, jealousy. A vintage I know well. But if you truly wanted her, you’d have already taken her—proper Nord tradition, isn’t it? Claim what’s yours before some pointy-eared bastard steals it. Sven: There are... ways to settle this. Like my forged letters, for one. Serana: Oh, adorable. You think seduction is won with ink? Tell me, have you ever even made a woman scream without plucking a string first? Sven: I—you—by Shor, what kind of demon are you?! Serana: The fun kind. Now, be a good boy and play us a song about heartbreak. Maybe it’ll be the first honest thing you’ve ever sung.

Sven: Enough of this... I'd rather drink with Orgnar than suffer your twisted tongue any longer. Serana: Run along then, little bard. But remember - if you ever want to learn seduction, my fangs are always available for lessons.
(The location is now Sleeping Giant Inn.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Daegon, Korpse, Lydia, Orgnar, delphine)

Jenassa: This inn's warmth cuts the night chill, but I keep my hand near my sword. Bandits lurk even in quiet places like Riverwood. Sven: Aye, the fire's a blessing after a long day, but the real warmth comes from a good tune. I've been strumming for Delphine and the patrons—keeps the shadows at bay, or so I tell myself. You look like you've seen your share of fights; care to share a tale over mead?

Jenassa: Settling into this chair eases the ache from the road, but my eyes stay sharp on that fire's glow—reminds me of campfires after a bloody hunt, warming the hands that just spilled blood. Tales, eh?

Sven: Aye, tales it is then. I've spun my share here at the Sleeping Giant—songs of dragons and lost loves under these very beams. But yours sound darker, forged in blood. What foes have you felled lately on these snowy roads? Jenassa: Dragons at High Hrothgar today. Greybeards droned on, but the beasts fell to arrows. Riverwood's too quiet for my taste—bandits could stir it up any moment.

Sven: That oven's heat beats the chill outside, but eavesdropping on Delphine's whispers—Thalmor parties and secret files? Sounds like a ballad waiting to be sung, full of intrigue and elf-hating spies. Jenassa: Thalmor scum deserve the blade, not fancy invites. If you're heading to Solitude for this embassy foolery, I'll tag along for the kills—bandits or elves, doesn't matter. Pay up, and my sword's yours against those golden-robed bastards.
Jenassa: Stocking up on potions and that Blades sword?
